title: JORS: prepare submission for lab_archives_mcp
JORS: address reviewer revisions and prepare resubmission
description: Umbrella epic for the Journal of Open Research Software revision round. Tracks manuscript corrections, response letter preparation, version/citation consistency, and final resubmission packaging required to answer the April 2026 reviewer and editor comments.
notes:
Recommended execution order:
1. Run lab_archives_mcp-2 and lab_archives_mcp-4 first in parallel. These are the evidence and metadata prerequisites that unblock later writing.
2. Revise the manuscript core in this order: lab_archives_mcp-5.2, lab_archives_mcp-5.3, lab_archives_mcp-5.1, then lab_archives_mcp-5.4 once the QC evidence from lab_archives_mcp-2 is available.
3. Treat lab_archives_mcp-5.5 and lab_archives_mcp-1.1.3 as optional polish after the compulsory reviewer items are covered.
4. Draft lab_archives_mcp-1.1.1 only after the required manuscript and metadata tasks are complete.
5. Leave lab_archives_mcp-1.1.2 as the final packaging and pre-upload step.
Estimated critical-path effort: roughly 10 to 12 hours excluding optional polish and upload time.

title: JORS: draft point-by-point response and cover letter
description: Draft the response-to-reviewers document and editor cover letter for the JORS resubmission. Address every reviewer point explicitly, identify where manuscript text changed, and justify any comment that is not addressed directly in the code or paper.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every reviewer and editor comment is addressed individually in the response document.
2. Each response states either what changed and where, or why no change was made.
3. The cover letter summarizes the main revisions without contradicting the detailed point-by-point response.
4. The response package is specific enough that an editor can map comments to manuscript or repository changes quickly.
notes:
Recommended order: 6. Estimate: 120 min. Start only after the required manuscript and metadata tasks are finished.

title: JORS: assemble revised upload package and submission checklist
description: Prepare the final resubmission package for upload to the JORS portal. Confirm the revised manuscript, figure assets, response-to-reviewers file, and any metadata or citation corrections are present and internally consistent before submission.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The revised manuscript, figures, and response-to-reviewers files are all present and named appropriately for JORS upload.
2. Version, citation, and DOI references are internally consistent across the uploaded materials.
3. The package includes the response document required by the editor and any revised figure assets referenced by the manuscript.
4. A final pre-upload check confirms there are no known missing compulsory reviewer items.
notes:
Recommended order: 7. Estimate: 45 min. Final step immediately before upload.

title: JORS: surface coverage reporting more clearly in README and CI summary
description: Optional improvement: make existing coverage collection and reporting more obvious in repository-facing documentation or badges so quality claims are easier for reviewers to verify.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Repository-facing documentation makes it obvious that coverage artifacts are already collected or explains clearly if they are not exposed publicly.
2. Any badge, README text, or CI-summary change introduced for this purpose remains accurate and non-inflated.
3. The optional improvement is kept separate from acceptance-critical JORS revisions if it cannot be completed in time.
notes:
Recommended order: optional. Estimate: 45 min. Do only if the acceptance-critical tasks are already covered.

title: Implement Graph-Based Knowledge Navigation Tools
description: Add tools like get_related_pages and trace_provenance to the MCP server. These should leverage the ExperimentContext and Finding models to allow the agent to traverse the semantic graph of the research.
design: Use NetworkX graph in ProjectContext for traversal. 'get_related_pages' will query graph neighbors. 'trace_provenance' will query graph predecessors.

comments.author: samuelbrudner
comments.text: NOTE: Terminology update. 'ExperimentContext' has been renamed to 'ProjectContext' to support multi-notebook workflows.

title: Implement 'Ready-to-Work' Heuristics
description: Create a suggest_next_steps tool that analyzes the current SessionState (active experiment, recent findings) to propose logical next actions for the agent.

comments.author: samuelbrudner
comments.text: NOTE: Terminology update. 'Experiment' references should be read as 'Project' (e.g., active project context).

title: Refine Semantic Model Definitions
description: Review and expand the ExperimentContext and Finding Pydantic models in state.py. Ensure they capture sufficient metadata (e.g., confidence scores, evidence links) to support the graph traversal and heuristic tools.

title: Integrate NetworkX for Graph Modeling
description: Add 'networkx' as a project dependency to support formal graph modeling of the ProjectContext (nodes=pages, edges=links). This will power the graph navigation tools.

title: Enforce real interrogate docstring coverage threshold
